comment.body:
*LVMH’s plunging share price and weak revenue are a big warning that the cheapest valuations in five years may not be enough to draw investors.*

The shares of the luxury giant have plummeted 35% from this year’s peak and are near the lowest forward-earnings multiple since the Covid era. Overall, the sector has lost 25% of its value in the past two months. And with the US and China digging in for a protracted trade war as the reporting season rolls on, the risk is that analysts will slash estimates even further, pressuring stocks.

comment.body:
SPX Game Plan for Wednesday, April 16
(Vixpery ATO + Mopex Thursday + $NVDA miss + China open to a deal)

---

CONTEXT
Tuesday Recap: SPX -0.2%, VIX -0.8 pts to 30.1%, 10Y yield -5bps. Tech (+0.4%) and Financials (+0.3%) led; Staples/Materials (-0.8%) lagged. Empire Manufacturing contracted but less than forecast.
Trump: “We may want countries to choose between us or China” — headline risk up.
$NVDA: Miss due to U.S. banning H20 chip exports to China. $5.5B expected Q1 charge. E-mini gapped down ~0.75%.
$ASML: Missed earnings → added pressure to semis.
Overnight EM (10 PM): IVx 30.1% = ±64.14 pts → range 5285.86–5414.14 around SPX 5350.
E-mini straddle (6 AM): ~75 pts → SPX range 5275–5425. Overnight low 5305, back to 5350.

---

KEY LEVELS & STRUCTURE (OI / GEX / TA–PA)
Call OI: 5400 = upside line-in-sand. Clusters to 5425. Size at 5500+.
Put OI: 5330 → clusters at 5295, 5275, 5250. NTM: 5365–5395 (dense zone).
GEX Clusters: 5225, 5250, 5275, 5300 (downside); 5350–60 (pivot); 5390/5400, 5420, 5450 (resistance).
WTD Range: High 5460 / Low 5355. 1-week = 4950–5480.
Over/Under pivot: SPX 5350 ≈ /ES 5400.

---

SPX ROAD MAP – 0DTE
Chop Zone: 5320–5395 (expect whipsaw behavior)
IF > 5350 → 5375/80
IF 5375/80 → test 5395 / battle 5400

Above Chop Zone:
IF > 5400 → 5420 → 5435/40 stall zone
IF > 5435/40 → 5450 retest (Mon. HOD)
IF > 5450 → 5470+ unlocks

Below Chop Zone:
IF < 5350 → 5330 → 5320 test (OVN low 5315)
IF < 5320 → 5300 (acceleration likely)
IF < 5300 → 5275 (stall/reverse zone)
IF < 5275 → 5250 → potential run toward 5200

---

VOLATILITY & POSITIONING INSIGHTS
GEX remains net negative → wide, fast moves
Earnings dispersion = vol suppression in spots + chop risk
Potential gamma/vanna resets midweek looks like it's not gonna happen?
$QYLD roll = low impact

---

ADDITIONAL CONSIDERATIONS
China deal rumors = headline tone softener (fragile)
Trump tariff rhetoric = ongoing tape bomb potential
MOVE Index high but easing → keep eyes on bond/vol feedback
VX and IV elevated = contagion risk in place
[7:42 AM]
WIDER ZONES
5200–5600 = Mopex range
5535–5550 = trigger zone 
5350 = structural pivot
5300 = weak shelf key level 
<5300 = vol event risk zone → dealer unwind in play

comment.body:
https://www.sec.gov/ix?doc=/Archives/edgar/data/0001045810/000104581025000082/nvda-20250409.htm

“The Company’s first quarter of fiscal year 2026 ends on April 27, 2025. First quarter results are expected to include up to approximately $5.5 billion of charges associated with H20 products for inventory, purchase commitments, and related reserves.

Certain statements in this Current Report on Form 8-K including statements regarding the USG’s intentions and the impact of the license requirement on the Company’s first quarter financial results are forward-looking statements that are subject to risks and uncertainties that could cause results to be materially different than expectations. Important factors that could cause actual results to differ materially include: global economic conditions; our reliance on third parties to manufacture, assemble, package and test our products; the impact of technological development and competition; development of new products and technologies or enhancements to our existing product and technologies; market acceptance of our products or our partners’ products; design, manufacturing or software defects; changes in consumer preferences or demands; changes in industry standards and interfaces; unexpected loss of performance of our products or technologies when integrated into systems; as well as other factors detailed from time to time in the most recent reports NVIDIA files with the Securities and Exchange Commission, or SEC, including, but not limited to, its annual report on Form 10-K and quarterly reports on Form 10-Q.”

This is a MASSIVE disclaimer list about earnings for Q1 potentially being drastically different than expectations. Not sure how to interpret it. But it doesn’t seem very positive 😂.

The only disclaimer they didn’t throw in there is a potential for a typhoon to hit TSM Taiwan manufacturing fobs.
